objectives: Review architectural design of mx20022-runtime Rust workspace
Evaluate trait design, dependency graph, 2PC implementation, context storage, coupling, scalability
constraints: PRD: No runtime abstraction over Tokio
PRD: Compile-time extension model for v1
important_facts: Duplicated Outcome enum in mx20022-runtime-core and mx20022-store
Context uses dyn Any + Send + Sync -- values not serializable, not persisted
Store persistence is not transactional (no SQL-level begin/commit)
Intermediate state transitions (Preparing, Committing) are not persisted
Feature flags defined but not enforced with #[cfg(feature)]
next_tx_id() is not globally unique across instances
INSERT OR REPLACE in begin_transaction silently overwrites
SQLite query() loads all rows and filters in Rust
channel_out parsed in config but never used in engine
No correlation engine implementation (only store trait surface)
risks_issues: Crash between process() and complete_transaction() leaves orphaned RECEIVED records: high risk
Duplicate tx_id overwrites via INSERT OR REPLACE: medium risk
Non-unique tx_id generation across instances: medium risk
dyn Any context blocks value auditability: medium risk

iteration.summary: Completed full architectural review of the mx20022-runtime workspace. The crate dependency graph is clean and acyclic with proper trait/implementation separation. The 2-phase commit orchestration is structurally correct. Critical concerns: duplicated Outcome enum, non-serializable dyn Any context values, non-atomic store persistence, unpersisted intermediate state transitions, and non-unique transaction IDs. Feature flags are defined but not enforced. Several PRD requirements (idempotency, correlation engine, dead letter replay, outbound channels) are not yet implemented.
outputs_produced: Structured architectural review with 5 critical concerns, 5 moderate concerns, 8 prioritized recommendations
next_actions: Add transactional scope to Store trait
